channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Digital chief: NHS AI ban is ‘criminal’
item.description: No NHS staff are allowed to use common AI tools – which is “criminal” and is encouraging “shadow” workarounds, a senior digital chief has said.

item.title: Revealed: Hospitals with the most ‘red line’ 24-hour waits
item.description: At least one in 10 A&E patients wait more than 24 hours at many hospitals, despite NHS England telling trusts to adopt a “zero tolerance” approach to such long waits, new figures have revealed.

item.title: ‘Innovation freeze’ threatening NHSE AI plans
item.description: NHS England has warned that it may be unable to lawfully deploy AI features on the NHS App from next year, due to incoming medical device regulation changes.

item.title: Long-serving deputy appointed acute trust’s CEO
item.description: An acute trust’s deputy chief executive has been appointed its next CEO, following a decade serving on the board.

item.title: Trust boards told to ‘grip’ cyber security
item.description: Trust boards must demonstrate they have “grip” of their cyber security, NHS England said.

item.title: Men earn more at 89% of trusts
item.description: The typical man earned 8 per cent more than the typical woman at nearly nine in 10 trusts last year, new figures show.

item.title: Ministers considering scrapping national NHS CEO
item.description: Ministers are considering abolishing the post of NHS chief executive to fully merge the management of the service with the health department, HSJ understands.

item.title: First ‘neighbourhood contract’ links GP income to A&E attendances
item.description: Some £1.7m of primary care revenue will be tied to A&E attendances from next year, under a first-of-its-kind “neighbourhood” contract deal.

item.title: Exclusive: Government plans US-style ‘hospitalist’ doctors
item.description: The government will introduce a new class of generalist hospital doctors – inspired by the American “hospitalist” role – by the end of the decade, according to leaked details of its upcoming workforce plan.

item.title: Government will be ‘incredibly lucky’ if current elective care plan works
item.description: The government and NHS England is wrong to think that improvements in productivity, and not increased funding, will result in shorter elective waiting lists, the leader of the NHS Alliance has claimed.

item.title: CEO departs trust with leadership ‘disconnect’
item.description: A trust grappling with concerns about leadership and culture has announced the retirement of its CEO.

item.title: Exclusive: NHSE pays three trusts £1m each to remove patients from waiting lists
item.description: Three trusts were paid more than £1m for removing patients from their waiting lists through “validation” exercises last year, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Influential DHSC panel leader resigns
item.description: The chair of a panel responsible for advising ministers on contested NHS service changes has resigned after six years in the job.

item.title: Stuck in the politician’s waiting room
item.description: Once again, the NHS is waiting. Waiting to see if there will be a new prime minister, waiting to see if they will appoint a new health secretary, waiting to see who that health secretary will appoint as Sir Jim Mackey’s successor. Waiting to see if the new leadership will ...

item.title: Deaths inquiry left trust unprepared for CQC inspection
item.description: A trust has claimed it was left unprepared for an unannounced Care Quality Commission inspection because of the demands of an inquiry into historic care failures.

item.title: Government accused of ‘disconnect’ in policy over new ICB role
item.description: The government’s decision to give integrated care boards partial responsibility for delivering a new £1.8bn special needs programme is creating “real tension” with their plans to cut staffing and focus on commissioning, system leaders have warned.

item.title: Vulnerable children denied surgery as cancer patients prioritised
item.description: Cancer waiting time pressures have led an acute trust to withdraw an offer to treat children with complex needs in its theatres.

item.title: Revealed: NHSE planning for £1.5bn local deficit
item.description: NHS England has finally revealed its planned local deficit figure for 2026/27, but admitted that ongoing negotiations mean it still has to find an additional £300m.

item.title: NHS accused of using suppliers as ‘free overdraft’
item.description: Overdue payments by trusts running to tens of millions of pounds have sparked a fresh row with suppliers, who have warned the situation is “not sustainable”.

item.title: CEOs given six months to take antisemitism training
item.description: Trust chairs and chief executives must take mandatory antisemitism and anti-racism training within six months, as part of efforts to tackle “routine ostracism” of Jewish people in the NHS.
